behavior_summary: Handles the 'U8P_GHOST::look' (inspect) interaction for the GHOST entity: when the player examines the ghost this script emits a single short bark reading "ghost " and then suspends the process.
There is no branching, menu interaction, flag reads/writes, or external calls recorded; the behavior is a simple descriptive response to an inspect action.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GHOST::look is executed as a process (process type 0x0208) tied to the engine's 'U8P_GHOST::look' event for this actor.
After emitting the Bark text "ghost ", the process issues a suspend which pauses/ends the U8P_GHOST::look handler (inferred from 'suspend' node).
No conditional logic or alternate execution paths are present in the function's node list.
open_questions: Why the bark text contains a trailing space ("ghost ") — likely harmless formatting or artifact; does it map to an in-game localized string entry?

Is the suspend intended to permanently pause the actor's U8P_GHOST::look process or simply yield briefly to engine scheduling? The script contains only 'suspend' so the exact lifecycle impact is inferred.
There are two low-confidence 'Unknown' nodes in the raw; their content does not appear to change behavior but could contain legacy/decompiler remnants.
